_grammar_commons.author: PlayfulProcess
_grammar_commons.description: Five-minute bedtime stories for ages 3-7, built from cross-cultural story arcs. Each book pairs two stories from different traditions around a shared theme.

title: The Campfire Stories
subtitle: Bedtime stories from around the world

items.title: Anansi and the Dark Cave
items.skill: Courage — bravery is not the absence of fear
items.refrain: Small legs, strong heart, one step more in the dark.

items.title: The Deer Who Stood Still
items.refrain: Ears up. Nose out. Breathe before you run.

items.title: The Rabbit in the Moon
items.skill: Generosity — giving from scarcity matters as much as giving from abundance
items.refrain: What I have, I give. What I give, I am.

items.title: The Girl Who Planted in the Ruins
items.skill: Cooperation — big problems require collective effort
items.refrain: Alone, too heavy. Together, we hold.

books.title: When the World Feels Too Big

items.title: How the River Found Its Way
items.skill: Tolerance for uncertainty — trusting the process without knowing the destination
items.refrain: I don't know the way, but I know how to move.

items.title: The Tanuki Who Carried Too Much
items.skill: Boundary-setting — saying no is a form of kindness
items.refrain: Not every load is mine to carry. Not every 'yes' is mine to give.

items.title: Anansi and the Stolen Rain
items.skill: Strategic thinking — unfair systems have structural weaknesses
items.refrain: Can't push the mountain? Go around it. Can't fight the tiger? Outsmart the pot.

items.title: The Girl Who Told the King a Story
items.skill: Emotional processing through narrative — stories heal what arguments cannot
items.refrain: A story ends when the heart opens. Not before.

items.title: The Girl Whose Hair Was Wild
items.skill: Self-acceptance — difference as gift, not deficit
items.refrain: What grows from you is part of you. What's part of you is not a mistake.

items.title: The Boy Who Thanked Everything
items.skill: Gratitude as attention — thanking as a practice of noticing and caring
items.refrain: We give thanks, and now our minds are one.
